data.title: Trade Desk: Buy Rating Reiterated as Publicis Partnership Resumes; Price Target Maintained at $35
data.description:
Analyst Youssef Squali from Truist Financial maintained a Buy rating on Trade Desk and keeping the price target at $35.00.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Youssef Squali has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to the renewed partnership between The Trade Desk and Publicis. He views the settlement of their dispute as an important catalyst, as it should restore ad spending from Publicis-affiliated brands that had been curtailed and had weighed on The Trade Desk’s recent revenue growth.

Squali also underscores that Publicis historically represented a meaningful share of The Trade Desk’s billings, so normalizing this relationship is strategically and financially significant despite uncertainties around the revised fee structure. In his view, this resolution, coming just ahead of key industry events, enhances The Trade Desk’s positioning with major advertisers, supporting his unchanged Buy rating and $35 price target.

In another report released on June 8, RBC Capital also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $33.00 price target.

TTD’s price has also changed dramatically for the past six months – from $36.650 to $19.280, which is a -47.39% drop .
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: MetaVia: Buy Rating Reiterated as Synergistic Cardiometabolic Pipeline Supports Unchanged $8 Price Target
data.description:
Jason McCarthy, an analyst from Maxim Group, maintained the Buy rating on MetaVia. The associated price target remains the same with $8.00.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Jason McCarthy has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, centered on MetaVia’s advancing cardiometabolic pipeline and newly reported synergistic combination data. He highlights that vanoglipel, an oral GPR119 agonist, showed compelling preclinical synergy when paired with resmetirom and metformin in MASH and obesity models, driving marked weight and fat mass reductions despite neither partner drug being a stand‑alone weight loss agent.

He also points to MetaVia’s broader platform, including DA-1726, a dual GLP‑1/glucagon agonist with ongoing Phase 1 work and an important titration readout expected in 4Q26, as a differentiated approach to increasing energy expenditure while managing appetite. In his view, the strength of the combination data, the focus on high‑value metabolic indications, and the optionality created by oral vanoglipel as a partner to other agents support the current valuation and justify maintaining an $8 price target and a Buy rating on MTVA shares.

According to TipRanks, McCarthy is an analyst with an average return of -14.4% and a 28.40% success rate. McCarthy covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Plus Therapeutics, Medicus Pharma Ltd, and Atossa Therapeutics.

In another report released on June 9, H.C. Wainwright also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $20.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
